title-group.article-title: Corrigendum to “Revaluating the dietary methionine requirements in an improved strain of Nile tilapia (Oreochromis niloticus)” [Heliyon 9 (3), (March 2023) Article e14376]

contrib-group.aff: Sustainable Aquaculture, WorldFish, Jalan Batu Maung, Batu Maung, 11960 Bayan Lepas, Penang, Malaysia

license.license-p: This is an open access article under the CC BY license (http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/).

related-article.article-title: Revaluating the dietary methionine requirements in an improved strain of nile tilapia (Oreochromis niloticus)

body.p: In the original published version of this article, the wrong reference list was published. To fix this, a corrected reference list will be published. The correct version of the reference list can be found below. The publisher apologize for the errors. Both the HTML and PDF versions of the article have been updated to correct the errors.

list-item.p: [1] NRC, Nutrient Requirements of Fish and Shrimp. National Academy Press, USA.2001.
[2] C.B. Santiago, R.T Lovell, Amino acid requirements for growth of Nile tilapia, The Journal of Nutrition. 118(12) (1988) 1540-1546. https://doi.org/10.1093/jn/118.12.1540

[3] H.B. Bentsen, B. Gjerde, N.H. Nguyen, M. Rye, R.W. Ponzoni, M.S.P. de Vera, H.L Bolivar, R.R. Velasco, R.R., J.C Danting, E.E Dionisio, F.M. Longalong, Genetic improvement of farmed tilapias: Genetic parameters for body weight at harvest in Nile tilapia (Oreochromis niloticus) during five generations of testing in multiple environments, Aquaculture. 338 (2012) 56-65. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.aquaculture.2012.01.027.

[4] R.W. Ponzoni, A. Hamzah, S. Tan, N. Kamaruzzaman, Genetic parameters and response to selection for live weight in the GIFT strain of Nile tilapia (Oreochromis niloticus), Aquaculture.247(1-4) (2005) 203-210.

[5] T. N. Nguyen, D. Allen Davis, Methionine Requirement in Practical Diets of Juvenile Nile Tilapia, Oreochromis niloticus, J World Aquacult Soc. 40(3) (2009) 410-416. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1749-7345.2009.00261.x.

[6] M. M. D. Castro, R. L. Albino, J. P. P. Rodrigues, A. L. L. Sguizzato, M. M. F. Santos, P. P. Rotta, J. S. Caton, L. E. F. D. Moraes, F. F. Silva, M. I. Marcondes, Energy and protein requirements of Holstein × Gyr crossbred heifers, Animal. 14(9) (2020) 1857-1866. https://doi.org/10.1017/S1751731120000622.
[7] W.K. Ng, N. Romano, A review of the nutrition and feeding management of farmed tilapia throughout the culture cycle, Reviews in Aquaculture. 5(4) (2013) 220-254.

[8] R. Yossa, N. Ahmad Fatan, J. Fairchild, J.W. Schrama, Apparent digestibility coefficients of local palm kernel cakes, rice bran, maize bran and sago flour in the GIFT strain of Nile tilapia (Oreochromis niloticus), Journal of Applied Aquaculture. 34(2) (2022) 502-526. https://doi.org/10.1080/10454438.2020.1869635.
[9] MS 2003, Animal Feeding Stuffs Determination of Moisture and Other Volatile Matter Content. (First revision) ISO 6496:1999, IDT.MS ISO 6496:2003. Department of Standard Malaysia, Malaysia, 2003.

[10] ISO 2009, Food and feed products-general guideline for the determination of nitrogen by the kjedahl method. International Organization for standardization, Vol. ISO 1871:2009. International Standard Organization, Geneva, Switzerland, 7, 2009.
[11] GAFTA. Oil Procedure A- Solvent Extraction for all feeding stuffs crude oil and fats Method 3:0, Grain and Feed Trade Association, London, England, 2014b.
[12] ISO 2002, Animal feeding stuffs - Determination of crude ash, ISO 5984:2002, International Standard Organization, Geneva ,Switzerland, 2002.
[13] GAFTA, Crude Fiber for all feeding stuff, Method 9:0. Grain and Feed Trade Association, London, England, 2014a.
[14] D. M. Sullivan, D. E. Carpenter, Methods of analysis for nutrition labeling. AOAC International, Arlington, VA,1993.
[15] AOAC 2005. Official method of Analysis. 18th Edition, Association of Officiating Analytical Chemists, Washington DC, 2005.

[16] R.A. M. Robbins, K. Saxton, L. L. Southern, Estimation of nutrient requirements using broken-line regression analysis1, Journal of Animal Science. 84(suppl_13) (2006) E155-E165. https://doi.org/10.2527/2006.8413_supplE155x.

[17] R. Yossa, M. Verdegem, Misuse of multiple comparison tests and underuse of contrast procedures in aquaculture publications, Aquaculture. 437 (2015) 344-350 https://doi.org/10.1016/j.aquaculture.2014.12.023.

[18] H. de Verdal, H. Komen, E. Quillet, B. Chatain, F. Allal, J. A. H. Benzie, M. Vandeputte, Improving feed efficiency in fish using selective breeding: a review, Rev Aquacult. 10(4) (2018) 833-851. https://doi.org/10.1111/raq.12202.

[19] C. Rodde, M. Vandeputte, T. Q. Trinh, V. Douchet, M. Canonne, J. A. H. Benzie, H. de Verdal, The Effects of Feed Restriction and Isolated or Group Rearing on the Measurement of Individual Feed Intake and Estimation of Feed Conversion Ratio in Juvenile Nile Tilapia (Oreochromis niloticus) for Selective Breeding Purposes, Frontiers in Genetics. 11(2021) 1790. https://doi.org/10.3389/fgene.2020.596521.
[20] Rotta PP, Marcondes MI and Engle T (2023) Editorial: Nutritional requirements in production animals. Front. Vet. Sci.10:1138636. doi: 10.3389/fvets.2023.1138636

[21] A. Arukwe, A. Goksøyr, Eggshell and egg yolk proteins in fish: hepatic proteins for the next generation: oogenetic, population, and evolutionary implications of endocrine disruption. Comparative Hepatology 2(1) (2003) 4 http://doi.org/10.1186/1476-5926-2-4.
